cell_id: 1992.sample_comment,"Irregular cliffs with wide stepped platforms upon which are coralline pools at varioaus levels and also very large blocks with small caverns underneath them containing pools. Some pools are interconnected by rills. The open rocks had a predominantly balanomytilus facies, with patches of rhodomytilus. Pools are coralline with thick encrustations bored by S. troglodytes. Algae were fairly abundant in the pools. The rills had a thick turf of red algae and/or a mat of H. elongata. The caverns were dominated by H. panicea and T. indivisa, together with hundreds of U. felina. A. equina hung from the roof. Pink O. nigra prowled under small boulders. The larger and deeper pools, mostly further into the bay, had a fringe of L. digitata and sandy floors. old_value: and thus had a thick cover of fucoids and other algae. The outside of the knob is exposed and had a balanomytilus cover. There are also coralline pools in which B. plumosa was found. Among interesting animals there was C. smithii and Actinia fragacea probably the most northern record. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system."
new_value: "At the site of investigation the lower shore is somewhat protected by Eilean a'Chaise and a separate smaller knob just to the south

cell_id: 66.sample_comment,"Loch Shader is a chain of almost closed lagoons
column_name: sample_comment,"Loch Shader is a chain of almost closed lagoons
old_value: Not well worked because of very heavy rain. Irregular rock platform with pools at base of cliff. Also many scattered rounded boulders. Most of the rocks and boulders are smoothed and flora and fauna is restricted to the pools except at LW. In these most growth is related to fringing red algae. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.
new_value: upper one almost fresh with causeway and sluice at mouth. Most of the loch has slightly reduced salinity and is very peaty. Few species but not special lagoon ones. Rapids/waterfall exit to sea about 3.5 m at ELWS. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system."

cell_id: 420.sample_comment,"Area studied was outwith main current, on the south side of the channel connencting Loch Roag ans East Loch Roag. A gentler slope than the north side with, at ELW, a gravel flat about 200 m longo and up to 50 m wide, with large and small boulders resting on it. Clean and very rich site. Upper shore is composed of cobbles passing down into an uninteresting middle shore with F. vesiculosus, A. nodosum and Littorina spp. The lower shore had a very rich F. serratus zone, passing down into an even richer extreme littoral fringe. The boulders supported a varied and dense turf of red algae including C. purpureum and P. rubens. Laminaria spp. grew on cobbles amongst the boulders and on M. modiolus in the gravel. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.","Area studied was outwith main current, on the south side of the channel connencting Loch Roag ans East Loch Roag. Upper shore is composed of cobbles passing down into an uninteresting middle shore with F. vesiculosus

cell_id: 1197.sample_comment,"The E side of Melbost Point is composed of conglomerate cut into an upper shore cliff which at the Pelvetia zone sharply flattens out into a rock platform. This extensive platform is stepped and cut by gullies and linear pools which on their upper side are often bordered by overhangs up to 1 m high. The shore was heavily covered in algae appropriate to the zone, with dense turfs of Rhodophyceae and wide zones of F. serratus leading down to a belt of L. digitata. A. plicata was common. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the originalsubstratum 1-3 scoring system."

cell_id: 994.sample_comment,"Irregular cliffs with wide stepped platforms upon which are coralline pools at various levels and also very large blocks with small caverns underneath them containing pools. Some pools are interconnected by rills. The open rocks had a predominantly balanomytilus facies, with patches of rhodomytilus. Pools are coralline with thick encrustations bored by S. troglodytes. Dominant animals were E. nebulosa and ascidians including C. lepadiformis. One M rostrata, decorated with C. rubrum to resemble A. bifida, was found. Mollusca included many juvenile P. exigum. Upper shore is composed of cobbles passing down into an uninteresting middle shore with F. vesiculosus, A. nodosum and Littorina spp. The lower shore had a very rich F. serratus zone, passing down into an even richer extreme littoral fringe. The boulders supported a varied and dense turf of red algae including C. purpureum and P. rubens. Laminaria spp. grew on cobbles amongst the boulders and on M. modiolus in the gravel. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system."

cell_id: 906.sample_comment,"Irregular cliffs with wide stepped platforms upon which are coralline pools at various levels and also very large blocks with small caverns underneath them containing pools. Some pools are interconnected by rills. The open rocks had a predominantly balanomytilus facies, with patches of rhodomytilus. Pools are coralline with thick encrustations bored by S. troglodytes. Algae were fairly abundant in the pools. The rills had a thick turf of red algae and/or a mat of H. elongata. The caverns were dominated by H. panincea and T. indivisa, together with hundreds of U. felina. A. equina hung from the roof. Pink O. nigra prowled under small boulders. The larger and deeper pools, mostly further into the bay, had a fringe of L. digitata and sandy floors. NOTE: Substratum % data are converted from the original substratum 1-3 scoring system.","Irregular cliffs with wide stepped platforms upon which are coralline pools at various levels and also very large blocks with small caverns underneath them containing pools.
